channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Minnesota justices set to settle questions of control in ‘dysfunctional’ House
item.description: After listening to GOP and DFL lawyers argue it out Thursday, the Minnesota Supreme Court could soon rule in a pair of cases that may shift the current power structure in the state House of Representatives.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump to visit disaster zones in North Carolina and California on first trip of second term
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is heading to hurricane-battered western North Carolina and wildfire-ravaged Los Angeles on Friday, using the first trip of his second administration to tour areas where politics has clouded the response to deadly disasters.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: March for Life returns to Washington: What to look for when anti-abortion activists gather
item.description: Thousands of anti-abortion activists are coming to Washington for the annual March for Life, seeking to build momentum after a string of victories and maintain pressure on legislators.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields, Christine Fernando And Ashraf Khalil, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Who's the guy handing Trump those binders of executive orders? Meet Will Scharf
item.description: Those binders full of executive orders that President Donald Trump has been signing with a flourish in recent days don’t just magically appear before him.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump orders release of JFK, RFK and MLK assassination records
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered the release of thousands of classified governmental documents about the 1963 assassination of President John F. Kennedy, which has fueled conspiracy theories for decades.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Utah Republicans take aim at teachers unions amid political clash over education
item.description: Utah lawmakers have advanced a bill that experts say would establish one of the most restrictive labor laws in the country.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Schoenbaum, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New York county agrees to redraw voting lines that lawsuit said disenfranchised residents of color
item.description: EAST MEADOW, N.Y. (AP) — A New York county has agreed to redraw its voting map after a lawsuit claimed its political boundaries disenfranchised residents of color.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz proposes $887 million bonding bill for infrastructure projects, not worried about Capitol impasse
item.description: Using the breached Rapidan Dam as a backdrop Gov. Tim Walz pitched his $887 million bonding bill. As well as funding for infrastructure, Walz wants to fund a new state patrol headquarters, and address housing and environmental needs.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Nevada state senator takes on gender, sports issue at UNLV, UNR, community colleges
item.description: Republican state Senator Carrie Buck is sponsoring legislation that would require Nevada colleges and universities to restrict participation in athletics based on the athlete's gender at birth.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says Ukraine's Zelenskyy is ready to negotiate a deal to end war with Russia
item.description: President Donald Trump told reporters Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is ready to negotiate a deal with Russia to end the "ridiculous war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate advances Pete Hegseth as Trump's defense secretary, despite allegations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate advanced the nomination of Pete Hegseth as President Donald Trump's defense secretary Thursday on a largely party-line vote, despite grave objections from Democrats and stirring unease among Republicans over his behavior and qualifications to lead the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US active duty troops beginning to arrive in Texas, San Diego to support border security
item.description: Active duty military troops will begin arriving in El Paso, Texas, and San Diego on Thursday evening, in what defense officials said is the first batch of the new forces being deployed to secure the southern border.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: JFK Files Declassified
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Miss America Abbie Stockard wore 'MAHA' dress designed by Andre Soriano to inaugural ball
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. was celebrated at a "MAHA" inaugural ball Jan. 20, 2025, where Miss America Abbie Stockard wore a custom "Make America Healthy Again" gown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pardons anti-abortion activists who blockaded clinic entrances
item.description: President Donald Trump announced Thursday he would pardon anti-abortion activists convicted of blockading abortion clinic entrances.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court allows small business registration rule to take effect, aimed at money laundering
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Thursday revived a requirement that owners of millions of small businesses register with an arm of the Treasury Department charged with fighting money laundering and other financial crimes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dems ask Trump USDA pick who will do 'backbreaking' farming amid mass deportations
item.description: Lawmakers want to know if President Donald Trump's plans of mass deportation for illegal immigrants will impact American farmers and create a worker shortage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FEMA faces shakeup under Trump while it wrestles with disasters on both coasts
item.description: President Donald Trump is preparing to reshape the Federal Emergency Management Agency, which has been on the frontlines of responding to disasters in California and North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump signs order to declassify files on JFK, RFK and MLK assassinations
item.description: President Donald Trump ordered files related to the assassinations of former President John F. Kennedy, Martin Luther King Jr. and Robert F. Kennedy be released.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Moderate GOP senator says she will vote against confirmation of Pete Hegseth for Defense Secretary
item.description: Moderte Sen. Susan Collins said she will not be backing Pete Hegseth for the role of secretary of defense under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks executive order ending birthright citizenship
item.description: A federal judge, John C. Coughenour, temporarily blocked President Donald Trump's executive order attempting to end birthright citizenship, citing it as blatantly unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Jason Nguyen, Robert Arnold, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump: Make products in America or face tariffs
item.description: “Come make your product in America, and we will give you among the lowest taxes of any nation on earth,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he may withhold federal aid for Los Angeles if California doesn't change water policies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump threatened to withhold federal disaster aid for wildfire-ravaged Los Angeles unless California leaders change the state's approach on its management of water.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: St. Paul City Council approves charter change to allow administrative fines
item.description: The St. Paul City Council on Wednesday voted to amend the city’s charter to allow it to impose fines for ordinance violations. It was one of few large cities in Minnesota that didn’t already have that power.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Senate panel overwhelmingly advances Trump pick for VA secretary
item.description: The Senate Veterans’ Affairs Committee on Thursday easily advanced former Georgia GOP Rep. Doug Collins's nomination to be the secretary of Veterans Affairs, setting him up for a full Senate vote likely to be uncontentious. The panel voted 18-1, with the lone "no" vote coming from Sen.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's interview with Sean Hannity
item.description: President Trump sat down Wednesday with Fox News host and ally Sean Hannity for his first one-on-one interview since returning to the White House for his second term. Trump has taken questions from reporters on each of his first three days in office, including the Hannity interview.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump suggests it was a mistake Biden didn't pardon himself
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday repeatedly suggested it was a mistake that former President Biden did not preemptively pardon himself before leaving office. Trump, in a sit-down interview with Fox News host Sean Hannity, also danced around questions on whether Biden should be investigated.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson forms committee to probe Jan. 6, Democrats' original investigation
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Wednesday said he will establish a new select subcommittee that will probe the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol riot — and likely dig into the now-defunct Jan. 6 select committee that was led by Rep. Bennie Thompson (D-Miss.) and then-Rep. Liz Cheney (R-Wyo.). Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump order halting refugee resettlement disrupting Minnesota agencies’ efforts
item.description: Officials with refugee resettlement agencies in Minnesota say Trump’s executive order halting the Refugee Admissions Program is disrupting efforts to bring people facing strife in their home countries to the U.S. and to the state.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: House Republicans seek to roll back DFL taxes, fees tied to transportation programs
item.description: Minnesota House Republicans are seeking to roll back taxes and fees approved when Democrats had full control of state government. A delivery fee and automatic increases to the gas taxes are on the GOP chopping block.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams: I'm 'at a loss' on how to handle migrants in NYC
item.description: Adams criticized the Biden administration for their border policies in an interview with Tucker Carlson.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Becker, NewsNation

item.title: Pritzker, Chicago officials vow to protect immigrant communities
item.description: Chicago's immigrant community and supporters are speaking out, fearful that families could soon be ripped apart.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, NewsNation

item.title: GOP congressman wants bishop 'added to deportation list' after Trump prayer service
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins heavily criticized statements made by the Right Rev. Mariann Budde on Tuesday at the inaugural prayer service held for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Trump to huddle with House GOP moderates at White House
item.description: A trio of moderate House Republicans are set to meet with President Trump at the White House on Wednesday, the latest huddle between the president and different GOP factions as the lower chamber prepares to pass his legislative agenda with its slim majority. Reps. Don Bacon (R-Neb.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson won’t ‘second-guess’ Trump pardons of violent Jan. 6 rioters
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) defended President Trump’s sweeping pardons of those who stormed the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, including numerous people who violently attacked police officers. “The president's made his decision. I don't second-guess those,” Johnson said at a press conference Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pardoned violent Jan. 6 rioters. Here are 5 of them
item.description: Hundreds of rioters accused of violently assaulting police at the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, were pardoned Monday by President Trump, despite remarks from his allies in recent weeks condemning those defendants.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5
